hero.name: Yash Raykar
hero.typingText: Frontend Intern Ready | MERN Developer | DSA Learner
hero.lead: Frontend-focused MERN developer building responsive interfaces, REST APIs, authentication flows, and database-driven systems. I am looking for a web development or frontend internship where I can contribute clean UI, reliable code, and fast learning.

automation.title: Batch-ready student portfolio generation from JSON.
automation.note: This section demonstrates how the same portfolio layout can map structured student records into reusable UI blocks for GEMA Clubs, LMS workflows, and future WordPress integration.

workflow.title: JSON Payload
workflow.text: Student records are stored in a structured JSON payload with student info, school info, participation data, documents, contact details, and portfolio sections.

workflow.title: Reusable Mapper
workflow.text: The renderer reads the JSON keys and maps them into consistent portfolio cards, academic blocks, certificate sections, document links, and contact panels.

workflow.title: Single UI Template
workflow.text: The visual design remains reusable while each student profile can change through data only, making bulk updates faster and less error-prone.

workflow.title: WordPress LMS Ready
workflow.text: The same data structure can be adapted for WordPress custom fields, LearnPress course progress, Gamify achievements, and certificate pages.

journey.title: Training, internship work, and project-based learning.
journey.note: The portfolio combines internship tasks, structured training, GitHub projects, and steady problem solving.

items.title: Web Development
items.text: Built responsive pages, React interfaces, Bootstrap layouts, reusable components, and Vite applications.

items.title: Backend APIs
items.text: Created Express.js REST APIs with JWT authentication, validation, protected routes, and structured controllers.

items.title: Database Practice
items.text: Worked with MongoDB, Mongoose, MySQL and CRUD operations.

items.title: MVC Architecture
items.text: Practiced project organization through routes, controllers, models, middleware, services, and utilities.

items.title: DSA Learning
items.text: Solved 150+ problems using Java across arrays, strings, recursion, searching, sorting, and logic building.

experience.title: Web Developer Intern at Codtech IT Solutions Pvt. Ltd.
experience.note: Current internship work adds real team workflow, code iteration, and production-style frontend practice to the project portfolio.

items.title: Web Developer Intern - Codtech IT Solutions Pvt. Ltd.
items.text: Develop reusable React.js components and responsive page sections, integrate REST APIs, resolve frontend bugs, test UI behavior across browser states, and maintain structured Git/GitHub updates from mentor feedback.

projects.title: Featured projects with code, UI evidence, and stack details.
projects.note: Projects show practical frontend, backend, database, authentication, API, and deployment experience.

items.title: VIP Engineer - Notes Selling Platform
items.description: Built a MERN-stack notes selling platform with JWT authentication, role-based access, PDF management, REST APIs, MongoDB Atlas integration, and deployment using Vercel and Render.
items.back: VIP Engineer is a full-stack platform where students can browse, purchase, and access study notes. Features include authentication, admin dashboard, note management, PDF uploads, and secure access control.

items.title: WanderLust Stays
items.description: Airbnb-style MERN project with property listings, user auth, reviews, owner controls, map location support, image uploads, and deployment on Render.
items.back: MERN travel listing app with authentication, reviews, owner features, maps, uploads, and deployed backend.

skills.title: Technical, academic, and professional skills.
skills.note: Skills are grouped around the resume target: frontend internships, MERN stack work, API integration, and DSA practice.

blog.title: Short notes on learning and project work.
blog.note: Concise developer notes that explain how I think about projects, practice, and continuous learning.

items.title: My Journey from Student to MERN Stack Developer
items.summary: I started with frontend basics, moved into backend routing and databases, and gradually connected both sides through full-stack projects.
items.more: This journey helped me understand how UI, APIs, validation, and databases work together in real applications.

items.title: What I Learned While Building Full Stack Projects
items.summary: Real projects taught me authentication, protected routes, schema design, API testing, validation, uploads, deployment, and debugging discipline.
items.more: The biggest lesson was that clean structure matters as much as adding features.

items.title: Why DSA and Projects Both Matter for Developers
items.summary: DSA improves structured thinking, while projects teach product flow, user experience, and practical engineering decisions.
items.more: Together, they build both interview confidence and practical engineering maturity.

testimonials.title: Signals from learning, review, and collaboration.

intro.label: Professional profile summary
intro.title: Hi, I am Yash Dilip Raykar.
intro.text: I am a final-year Computer Engineering student specializing in AI, a Web Developer Intern at Originedge Technology, and a frontend-focused MERN developer. I build responsive interfaces, REST API workflows, authentication-based apps, and database-driven projects, with 150+ DSA problems solved and 15+ GitHub repositories.

quotes: Build quietly. Let the projects speak.
Great software starts with clear thinking.
DSA sharpens logic. Projects sharpen judgment.
